**Acceptance Criteria**

Blocking: external plan files are resolved and tested only in the new helper module, but production validation does not use the resolver. [crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs:229) adds `project_plan_doc`, but `rg` shows no callers outside its own unit tests. The graph validation path still calls `criterion_ids(container, ...)`, and `criterion_ids` parses `source.description` directly at [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:760) and [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:768). `CommandExecutor::evaluate_graph_rules` passes only `issues` plus `repo_format` into `evaluate_graph` at [crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:405), with no `[planning].plan_doc_location` or repo root boundary context. Result: `plan_doc_location = "dev/active/{id}-plan.md"` is ignored by actual validation, so the issue’s core criterion, “loads a plan document from either the issue body or a configured external path and feeds its content to the pure validation engine,” is not met.

The unit test at [crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs:340) proves the helper can project identical section content, but it does not prove that `jit validate` or graph rules consume external plans.

**Architecture / Separation Of Concerns**

The new filesystem read is in `commands/plan_doc.rs`, which is a boundary layer, and the existing domain projection remains pure. However, because the resolver is not wired into the command validation path, the intended boundary-to-engine flow is incomplete.

**Functional Style**

No blocking style issue in the added resolver. The implementation is mostly expression-oriented and scoped.

**Correctness / Invariants**

No writes, event mutations, gate state transitions, assignee/label parsing, PID handling, or unsafe code are introduced by the target commit. The correctness issue is functional integration: external plan content cannot affect validation.

**Testing**

Provided issue evidence shows `cargo-ci` passed. I also ran:

```text
./scripts/jit-validate.sh
cargo test -p jit plan_doc --quiet
cargo test -p jit test_inline_and_external_project_identical_content --quiet
```

All passed; `jit-validate` emitted allowed label warnings. Test coverage is still insufficient for the acceptance criteria because there is no command/harness or validation-path test showing configured external plan content is used by graph validation.

**Error Handling**

`PlanDocError::Read` includes the issue id and resolved path at [crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs:68), satisfying the helper-level missing/unreadable path criterion. Since production validation never calls this path, missing external plan files will not surface as validation errors.

**CLI Contract**

No CLI command was added. Existing `--json` behavior is not changed.

**Documentation**

Most new public APIs include examples. Minor issue: public `INLINE_LOCATION` at [crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/plan_doc.rs:32) has docs but no `# Examples`, contrary to the review checklist. This is secondary to the integration failure.

**Prior Review Feedback**

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior feedback to verify.

**Jit Issue Management**

The prerequisite dependency `c78168d8` is marked `done`. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed with label warnings only.

VERDICT: FAIL
